source.title: Discovering Event Structure in Continuous Narrative Perception and Memory

confidence_rationale: Foundational direct evidence for hierarchical event segmentation with nested timescales: short events in early sensory cortex, long events in mPFC/posterior cingulate. HMM method is itself a substrate-algorithm candidate. High mapping fidelity for trigger criterion (b) latent change-point and for question 2 hierarchy. Boundary detection at high-order regions co-fires with hippocampal activity -- direct relevance to MECH-287 coupling.
failure_signatures: Short-timescale regions (early visual) reported many short events; long-timescale regions (mPFC, PMC) reported few long events. A flat single-segment-id substrate would either over-segment if tuned to fast regions or under-segment if tuned to slow ones.

High-order event boundaries triggered hippocampal activity. A substrate where the event_segmenter does NOT couple to the hippocampal anchor system would miss this biological coupling and break MECH-287 trigger semantics.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Cortical activity during continuous narrative perception is naturally structured into a nested hierarchy of events: short events in early sensory regions, long events in high-order areas. High-order event boundaries are coupled to increases in hippocampal activity that predict later free-recall reinstatement.
mapping.ree_translation: Two architectural recommendations follow. (a) The event_segmenter should support multiple timescales -- a single segment_id stream is biologically inadequate; a hierarchical segment_id (segment.outer.inner) is the closer match. (b) Event boundaries at the integrative scale should drive hippocampal anchor system activity -- this directly supports MECH-287 broadcast trigger as DOWNSTREAM of MECH-288 boundary detection at integrative timescales (the (c) coupling option in the spec).
mapping.mapping_caveat: Baldassano's hierarchy emerges from cortical region anatomy in fMRI; the substrate's hierarchy must emerge from architectural choices (which streams are watched, with what time constants). The substrate cannot inherit specific timescales from this paper without empirical tuning.
mapping.source_context: fMRI of healthy human adults watching/listening to narratives (BBC Sherlock + audio narrative); HMM-based event segmentation.

confidence_components.notes: Neuron paper, widely cited, methodologically rigorous. Mapping high because hierarchical timescale finding maps cleanly onto MECH-288 design question 2; HMM method is one of substrate-algorithm candidates.
